goldEvidence.quote: .documentsDirectory.appending(path: "NavigationPath.json") - Used to store navigation path data in the Documents directory.

Use `ForEach` to provide views based on a RandomAccessCollection of some data type. Either the collection's elements must conform to Identifiable or you need to provide an `id` parameter to the `ForEach` initializer.
Either the collection's elements must conform to Identifiable or you need to provide an `id` parameter to the `ForEach` initializer.

Present HTML, CSS, and JavaScript content alongside your app's native views with WebView. Specify web content with a URL when using the init(url:) initializer, or with a WebPage when using the init(_:) initializer.

A standard label for user interface items, consisting of an icon with a title. You create a label, in its simplest form, by providing a title and the name of an image, such as an icon from the SF Symbols collection: Label("Lightning", systemImage: "bolt.fill")
Whether the system preference for Reduce Motion is enabled. If this property's value is true, UI should avoid large animations, especially those that simulate the third dimension.

Use `ImageRenderer` to export bitmap image data from a SwiftUI view. You initialize the renderer with a view, then render images on demand, either by calling the render(rasterizationScale:renderer:) method, or by using the renderer's properties to create a CGImage, NSImage, or UIImage.
Using the Attribute macro with the unique option ensures that the `id` property is unique: @Attribute(.unique) public var id: String
Learn how to use @Query, @Bindable, .modelContainer, the .modelContext environment variable, and DocumentGroup to integrate with the SwiftData framework.

By using a PersonNameComponents/FormatStyle, you can bind the text field to a nonstring type, using the format style to convert the typed text into an instance of the bound type. The following example uses a PersonNameComponents/FormatStyle to convert the name typed in the text field to a PersonNameComponents instance.

Layers the views that you specify in front of this view. Use this modifier to place one or more views in front of another view. For example, you can place a group of stars on a RoundedRectangle using overlay(alignment:content:).
